utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , edward , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well , yourself ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: absolutely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: edward is a 59 year old male with a past medical history significant for depression , hypertension and prior rotator cuff repair who presents for followup of his chronic problems . so , edward , it's been a little while since i saw you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing pretty well , actually . it's been a good , uh , good six months .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . okay . so , you know , the last time we spoke , you know , you were trying to think of some new strategies to manage your depression . you did n't wan na go on medication because you're already on a bunch of meds .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: absolutely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , how are you doing with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well . i see a counselor , uh , once a week . uh , and i've been out swimming at the pool a lot this , this , uh , summer , and , uh , fall . so , things have been well , going well with my depression .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so , you do n't wan na , you do n't feel the need to start any medications at this time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no , no . but , i know i can call you if i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , absolutely .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . all right . and then , in terms of your high blood pressure , how are you doing with that ? i know we , we were kind of struggling with it la- six months ago . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i still have my good days and my bad days . i do take my medicine daily . uh , but , you know that burger and wine , every once in a while , sneaks in there , and that salt be ... we know what that does .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . so , i love burgers and wine too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i get it . um , okay , so , and you're taking the norvasc ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: norvasc , yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and , you're checking your blood pressures at home , it sounds like ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i , i do . well , i go to cvs pharmacy . they , they have a , uh , machine that i can sit down at quickly and get my , uh , blood pressure taken . and , i go there about once a week .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and then , i know that you had that rotator cuff repaired about eight months ago . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it's doing well . i , i'm , i'm , been stretching with a yoga ball .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh- .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and , uh , i'm getting stronger each time . and , i can continue that once a week also .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . are you still seeing the physical therapist in the center , or are you just doing exercises at home ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm just , i progressed to exercises at home .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . great . all right . well , i know you did a review of systems sheet when you checked in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and , you know , it seems like you're doing well . any symptoms at all ? any nasal congestion or chest pain , shortness of breath , anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . none of those . i do , do notice that i get a little bit of a de- , uh , swelling in my ankles .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , mainly near the end of the day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it seems to go away by the next morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . all right . maybe that has to do with some of the salt intake you're , you're eating .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well , i wan na go ahead and do a quick physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? show me the blood pressure . yeah , so , your blood pressure's a little elevated today , 156 over 94 .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you know , you could be a little happy to see me . i do n't know .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , but let's look at some of the readings . hey , dragon ? show me the blood pressure readings . yeah , so , they've been a , running a little high over the past couple months .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we'll have to just kinda talk about that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na go ahead and listen to your heart and lungs , and i'll let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so , on physical exam , you know , everything looks good . on your heart exam , i do appreciate a slight three out of six systolic ejection murmur , but we've heard that in the past .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , that seems stable to me . um , on your lung exam , everything sounds nice and clear , and on your lower extremity exam , i do appreciate , you know , 1 to 2+ pitting edema in your legs , okay ? so , we'll have to just talk a little bit about your diet and decreasing the salt intake , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let me just look at some of your results , okay ? hey , dragon ? show me the labs . so , looking here at your lab results , everything looks really good . you know , your creatinine , that's your kidney function , that looks stable . everything looks good from that standpoint . hey , dragon ? show me the ekg . and , looking here at your ekg , everything , you know , looks fine . there's no evidence of any coronary artery disease . it's a nice , normal ekg , which is good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let me just talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you . okay ? so , from a depression standpoint , it's , you know , your first problem , i think that that sounds like you're doing really well managing it . you know , you have good strategies . it sounds like you have a good support system , um , and i agree . i do n't think you need to start on any medication at this time , but you said before , you know you can call me , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your second problem , your hypertension , i , i do n't believe it's well controlled at this time . so , i wan na go ahead and , you know , increase the norvasc up to 10 mg a day , and i wan na go ahead and order an echocardiogram and a lipid panel , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon ? order an echocardiogram . and , for your third problem , your rotator cuff repair , i , i think you're doing really well with that . i would just continue with the exercises and , uh , i do n't think we need to intervene upon that anymore . it sounds like that's pretty much resolved , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have any questions about anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no questions .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . hey , dragon ? finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 59-year-old male with a history of depression, hypertension, and prior rotator cuff repair.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient is managing his depression.
The patient attends counseling weekly and swims regularly to manage his depression.
The doctor asks if the patient feels the need to start antidepressant medication.
Hypertension
The patient reports variable blood pressure control and occasional dietary indiscretions involving wine and burgers.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his Norvasc.
The doctor asks if the patient is monitoring his blood pressure at home.
The patient checks his blood pressure at a CVS pharmacy approximately once weekly.
Rotator cuff repair
The patient is doing well and performing yoga ball stretches.
The doctor asks if the patient is still attending formal physical therapy.
The patient has progressed to performing exercises at home.
The doctor asks about symptoms including nasal congestion, chest pain, and shortness of breath.
The patient denies nasal congestion, chest pain, and shortness of breath, but reports ankle swelling.
The patient reports ankle swelling occurs mainly at the end of the day.
The patient reports the ankle swelling resolves by the next morning.
The patient's blood pressure is elevated at 156/94 mmHg.
Physical exam reveals a stable 3/6 systolic ejection murmur.
Physical exam reveals clear lungs and 1-2+ pitting edema in the lower extremities.
Laboratory results show normal creatinine and a normal EKG with no evidence of coronary artery disease.
The doctor confirms the patient is managing depression well and agrees to continue without medication.
The doctor increases Norvasc to 10 mg daily.
The doctor orders an echocardiogram and a lipid panel.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue with home exercises for the rotator cuff.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.

case_description: chronic problems; depression;hypertension;hx rotator cuff repair
